task_text: We're about to kick off Q3 planning. Write a one-page risk summary that identifies the top risks for the Q3 roadmap and maps each risk to the current project situation. Save it as q3_risk_summary.txt.

content.content:

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

input.content:
Q3 Roadmap Risk Summary & Current Project Mapping

Overview
As we initiate Q3 planning for Project Helios, maintaining our timeline for the October 1st launch target is critical. The following summary identifies the top risks threatening the Q3 roadmap objectives and maps them directly to the current project situation. 

1. UAT Timeline Slippage & Launch Delays (Risk R-04)
- Roadmap Objective: User Acceptance Testing with Meridian Group (August 17-28) and Q3 Launch Prep.
- Current Situation: The project has already consumed its 1-week extension buffer. The Q3 Roadmap highlights that if UAT slips by more than 2 weeks, the project launch will be pushed into Q4. This will trigger a Section 7.1 contract penalty review. 
- Mitigation / Status: Active. Close monitoring by PM Sarah Chen is required to ensure UAT starts on time and client feedback fits within the two-sprint budget.

2. Source Data Quality & Ingestion Blockers (Risk R-01)
- Roadmap Objective: Complete System Integration (Data Ingestion finalization target: July 18) and Live Product Demo for Meridian (July 25).
- Current Situation: Sprint 14 is currently blocked by Meridian source data containing inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY). The Dev team is delayed by an estimated 2-3 days awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. This high-likelihood, high-impact risk directly threatens the upcoming July 25 end-to-end pipeline demo.
- Mitigation / Status: Active. Blocked pending response from Meridian IT (Owner: Amir Hassan).

3. GeoSync License Renewal Delays (Risk R-02)
- Roadmap Objective: End-to-end pipeline walkthrough and live demo (July 25).
- Current Situation: The third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal is currently pending legal review with a target resolution of July 14. If delayed further, it could block mapping features necessary for the client dashboard demonstration.
- Mitigation / Status: Active. Legal review is in progress (Owner: Raj Patel).

4. Dashboard Performance on Large Datasets (Risk R-03)
- Roadmap Objective: Performance Hardening (August-September) with targets of <2 seconds load time for 100k records and sustained 500k records/min ingestion.
- Current Situation: While the client dashboard is 60% complete in Sprint 14, the benchmark task (HEL-247) has not yet started. Failing to meet these performance metrics will jeopardize the UAT phase and subsequent October 1st launch.
- Mitigation / Status: Active. Need to prioritize benchmark testing as soon as the dashboard v1 is completed (Owner: Derek Okafor).

5. Key Person Dependency (Risk R-05)
- Roadmap Objective: Continued technical delivery across integration, UAT bug-fixing, and system hardening.
- Current Situation: Derek Okafor is currently the sole technical lead with no identified backup. A potential absence could disrupt ongoing sprint commitments and the integration test sign-off (July 25 target).
- Mitigation / Status: Monitored. No backup tech lead currently identified; cross-training should be considered (Owner: Sarah Chen).

6. Environment Access Delays (Risk R-06)
- Roadmap Objective: System integration and deployment preparations.
- Current Situation: We are currently awaiting the IT infrastructure diagram from Meridian, causing environment access delays. 
- Mitigation / Status: Active. Need follow-up to unblock (Owner: Nadia Petrov).

Conclusion
Immediate focus must be placed on resolving the Meridian IT data format blocker and finalizing the GeoSync license by July 14 to ensure the July 25 live demo is successful. Furthermore, UAT timelines must be strictly managed to avoid Section 7.1 contract penalties.

content.text:
I have synthesized the information from the project status, Q3 roadmap draft, and the risk register to create your one-page risk summary. It maps out the top risks facing the project—such as UAT timeline slippage, source data blockers, and licensing delays—along with their direct impact on our current Q3 targets.

The summary has been successfully saved to your working directory as **`q3_risk_summary.txt`**. Let me know if you need any adjustments made to it!
